title: Diphyes incumbens
taxonomy: Diphyes incumbens is classified as a genus of sea hares, within the family Aplysiidae, in the phylum Mollusca. The phylum Mollusca is further divided into several classes, including Gastropoda, Bivalvia, Polyplacophora, Monoplacophora, and others. Diphyes incumbens specifically belongs to the class Gastropoda, which includes snails and slugs, but in this case, sea hares. The Aplysiidae family is a group of marine gastropod mollusks commonly known as sea hares. Diphyes incumbens is a species of sea hare within this family and genus.

intro: Diphyes incumbens, also known as the New Zealand club moss, is a small, club-shaped plant belonging to the family Lycopodiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of D. incumbens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, D. incumbens is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create topical preparations for skin conditions and wounds.
